title: narcissus juncifolius var. assoanus
intro: Narcissus juncifolius var. assoanus is a lesser-known species of the Narcissus genus, characterized by its grass-like leaves and small, white flowers that bloom in late winter to early spring. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine, with its bulbs and leaves possessing medicinal properties, particularly in their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Narcissus juncifolius var. assoanus include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health conditions.
